title: Session Tracking Mechanisms in Java Web Applications
description: A flowchart illustrating the interaction between a client browser, a JSP page, and the server, demonstrating the primary methods of session tracking (Cookies, URL Rewriting, and HttpSession object).
relationships: Server creates a new HttpSession and generates a unique JSESSIONID
Server sends response back to client, embedding JSESSIONID via a Cookie or URL Rewriting
Client sends subsequent requests including the JSESSIONID
Server uses the incoming JSESSIONID to find the existing HttpSession and retrieve user-specific data
